title: Phenomenon 36
wikitext:
{{VoidedTheme|mono}} {{Blurbackground|file=Black-Screen.png}} {{CSS|content=@import url('https://fonts.googleapis.com/css2?family=Fjalla+One&family=New+Amsterdam&family=Whisper&display=swap');}} {{DISPLAYTITLE:The labels the world give us pale in comparison to our own}} __NOTOC__
Phenomenon 36: '''Tattoos'''
{{Quote|1=Scars have the strange power to remind us that our past is real.|2=Cormac McCarthy}} [[File:TattooImg1.png|right|300px]]
'''Figure 1''' - Dana Rivers Dana aquired this tattoo on the 31st of December, 2022, reportedly she had acquired the tattoo through the massive parties staged by [[Backroom Colonists|The Backrooms Colonists]] during the turn of the year, one that she found great joy in. As she's stated, the staff-like design represents the fireworks going up into the skin, then bursting. According to her, she plans to take more permanent efforts to embed it into her skin, planning to leave it as a permanent tattoo to signify her time in the Backrooms.
'''Phenomenon 36''', also known as "Tattoos" are a collective, sudden, change of melanin produced on a wanderer's body. These are largely attributed to the passing of DNA between wanderers, especially after prolonged exposure to one another and often indicative of great emotional discourse or display. Beyond these changes, there appears to be no difference from a wanderer with the Tattoos genome and one without it, unless it has already produced tattoos. There is no known "Patient 0" of Phenomenon 36, with many sure it was always a feature within the Backrooms.

Tattoos appear no different to the standard type of those found within the Frontrooms, most of which created in an entirely black "ink" that shares a perfect appearance to generic brand tattoo ink. Additionally, Phenomenon 36 behaves similarly to tattoos in other ways too, with fading of the marking taking a period of roughly 7 years, though can be removed earlier through laser treatment. However, due to the lack of such programs in the Backrooms, many are left to wait for 7 year period for them to be removed naturally through cell replacement.

What does the unfathomable feel like? The sensation of something you have yet to understand. A memory washing over your skin, like small pins poking holes into our fallible bodies. It's the prickles of the sun casting it's light over skin, or at least the memories of it. It's the chills as you recall your life being saved, dancing on the brink of death but returning back alive. It's the movies of memories, playing back as the scene is rehearsed again. And again. And again.
[[File:TattooImg2.png|left|400px]]
'''Figure 2''' - August Lee Due to the aftermath of his experience on [[Our Hallowed Nest|Hallowed Nest]], August found a tattoo on his arm resembling a starfish. Initially assuming it to be one out of spite, August spoke on that he really wanted to remove it for a long period, due to it bringing up bad memories. As of currently, the tattoo has faded away completely, and when asked, his opinion on it has shifted largely neutral, instead of negative. As a major event happens to a wanderer, the brain is recorded to transmit signals across to body to respond accordingly. If the wanderer is affected by the strand, signals will be sent to certain parts of the skin as well, encouraging the rapid use and development of melanin and from there, Tattoos. The explanation for why the body does this is unknown, however there are some consistencies. Larger and more emotional events will receive larger Tattoos, and no matter where they are placed, it'll be removed after 7 years of it first developing. Additionally, all Tattoos depict a loose interpretation of the events, and wanderers with them will feel compelled to provide such explanation for them if asked about it. While the spreading of Tattoos is well documented, the goals of it remain unknown. There appears to be no benefit to the DNA if it is passed on to others, nor is it alive, meaning a new host is not providing any sort of sustenance for it. Others have assumed it to be a made-up phenomenon entirely, as some point to humanity's urge to misconstrue details and events in order to service them. Because of this, wanderers will adorn tattoos from an unknown source to help them stand out or seem more interesting and sympathetic to those around them. There have been no Backrooms-based tattoo artists that have taken ownership of these. With so few of them as well, the widespread nature of this phenomenon makes the theory difficult to prove.

The body changes all the time, it's how vitality is kept and cancer is fought. Tumors appear, then fade away again. pushed back by the machine that is man. The resetting of cells keeps us safe and strong. Despite how strong it was, that's the worst it will be. For every day the body chips away, replacing what is old with what is new. There will always be change. So why don't we embrace that change? Every day 330 billion cells die. 1 percent of the flesh is gone after 24 hours. There's always time for a change. A step forward. The cells that run all mankind only stop when the body dies. We truly are infinite to the mindless pieces of our flesh.
[[File:Tattoo3.png|right|400px]]
'''Figure 3''' - James Patterson Acquired in the aftermath of his time in [[Level 830|Level 830]], James has spent a while recounting his initial fear and displeasure with the tattoo, especially regarding seeing the repetitive death of his father, Leonardo Patterson. When he was asked about what helped him learn to love the tattoo, he often attributed it to the varying compliments and support he got from it, especially as he'd go on to talk about the impact it had on him more extensively. While not thankful, the appearance of it was a step in the direction of healing, as he claimed. Tattoos are a sign of change in ones life. In esoteric forms they detail what a wanderer has seen and survived. The good, the bad, and everything between and beyond those extremes. It is an artifact of the skin and mind to regale these happenings to many wanderers with Tattoos. However, it's been shown that through their time, stuck with this marking, many have grown more at peace with those events. Some have grown to not miss the times that Tattoos shows, others learned to accept the change upon their life Tattoos remind them of. But of course, after 7 years, all the cells in the wanderer's body has been replaced, and Tattoos will fade alongside it. This presents the wanderer with a sort of symbol. As after the Tattoo fades, the gene will die, and those afflicted may never see it again. The responses of those after its disappearance have been oddly reminiscent, as some have spoke on it helping them learn from their life and strive to grow. And for some, it was more akin to a scar fading away, leaving them with something untouched and new.

{{Collapse|num= |final= |initial= Tattoos were never about the genes |body=Tattoos were never about the genes We live our lives chasing after highs A new experience to be had, a new sight to see Some of us rely on photography to capture the moment A moment so fleeting you already passed it Other want to record those seconds Promising an infinite loop of memory Some have things they wish to shed away Burn out of their minds, a foul record Tattoos are our open secrets No more common than the words we speak Everyone has their "tattoos" Memories that define their lives Thoughts of who to be and where they come from Our mind is usually our tattoo A marking of the person, who they are Tattoos aren't a piece of DNA They are something that can just appear They are our memories Printed out in a way our brain can understand But others don't That's why we ask And that's why they tell The record of our memory will always spin Despite the growth of the body We never really change on the inside What makes us human never differs or falters It's eternal So the memories we all keep May soon die like the tattoos on our skin Our humanity will never fade As long as there are tattoos to be made Blemishes of good and evil There is a human inking the skin There is a mind holding it tightly There is a piece of humanity looking to it Watching Remembering For infinity}}
